content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected weighted graph it has NNN vertices and MMM edges. 

You are tasked to find the length of the shortest path duvd_{uv}duv​ between each pair of vertices uuu and vvv.

TaskImplement the Floyd-Warshall Algorithm to solve the problem


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a two integers NNN and MMM, denoting the number of vertices and edges.
Next MMM lines describes the edges. i−thi-thi−th of these MMM lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​ and wiw_iwi​ denoting there exists an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ with weight wiw_iwi​.


Output Format
Output a Matrix of N x N dimension - i−thi-thi−th  line has the distance from node iii to all the other nodes.
If there exists no path from iii to jjj output -1 instead.
content.inputFormat: First line contains two integers N and M, denoting the number of vertices and edges. Next M lines describe the edges. Each of these M lines contains three space-separated integers u, v, and w, denoting there exists an edge between u and v with weight w.
content.outputFormat: Output a Matrix of N x N dimension - the i-th line has the distance from node i to all the other nodes. If there exists no path from i to j, output -1 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The shortest path from node 1 to node 2 is directly through the edge with weight 5. The shortest path from node 1 to node 3 is through node 2 with a total weight of 8.
The shortest path from node 1 to node 3 is through node 2 with a total weight of 2.
The shortest path from node 1 to node 2 is directly through the edge with weight 1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the Floyd-Warshall Algorithm, which is an algorithm for finding the shortest paths in a weighted graph with positive or negative edge weights. The algorithm works by considering all possible paths of length 1, 2, ..., n, where n is the number of vertices in the graph. For each pair of vertices, it checks if there is a shorter path by going through an intermediate vertex.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) per test case, where N is the number of vertices. This is because the Floyd-Warshall Algorithm has a time complexity of O(N^3).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the adjacency matrix of size N x N.
